zh.lifeSummary: Thomas 1Er Dit De Saint Valéry DE DOMART於1173年8月8日出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆沙吉瓦萊-素-頌。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1214年8月10日在法國豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德布文斯享年41歲。

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: 1Er
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Yiddish ber ‘bear’, probably influenced by the early medieval European practice of giving animal names to people. It is often paired with Dov in order to provide a Hebrew name in certain rituals.

nameMeanings.name: Dit
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Dorothy .

variants.name: Dorothy
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: name bestowed in honour of the medieval poet Dante Alighieri ( 1265–1321 ), also used in the English-speaking world. As a medieval given name Dante was a contracted form of Durante ‘steadfast, enduring’. In an English context it was famously borne by the pre-Raphaelite poet and painter Dante Gabriel Rossetti ( 1828–1882 ), the son of an exiled Italian scholar and patriot.

nameMeanings.name: Valéry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Masculine form of   Valérie  , which has absorbed an older name of Germanic origin composed of elements meaning ‘foreign power’.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas 1Er Dit De Saint Valéry DE DOMART was born on 8 August 1173, in Saint-Valéry-sur-Somme, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France. He had at least 1 son. He died on 10 August 1214, in Bouvines, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas 1Er Dit De Saint Valéry DE DOMART est né le 8 août 1173, à Saint-Valéry-sur-Somme, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 10 août 1214, à Bouvines, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas 1Er Dit De Saint Valéry DE DOMART wurde am 8. August 1173 in Saint-Valéry-sur-Somme, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 10. August 1214 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Bouvines, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas 1Er Dit De Saint Valéry DE DOMART è nato il il 8 agosto 1173 nel Saint-Valéry-sur-Somme, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 10 agosto 1214, nel Bouvines, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia, all'età di 41 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas 1Er Dit De Saint Valéry DE DOMART(은)는 1173년 8월 8일 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 솜 세인트-발e리-수-소에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1214년 8월 10일 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 노르드 부빈스에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas 1Er Dit De Saint Valéry DE DOMART родился 8 августа 1173 г. в местности сеинт-валeри-сур-сомм, сомм, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 10 августа 1214 г. в местности боуваинесь, норд, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 41 год.
